codeReview.summary: The SSE implementation is substantial and covers most progressive rendering behavior, but it introduces a blocking regression in auto-refresh integration and has a cancellation lifecycle risk.

issues.description: Auto-refresh start condition is broken: `runCompareAndMaybeStartAutoRefresh()` checks `hasQuoteResults(comparison.payload)`, but the progressive path resolves `{ ok: true, params }` without `payload` (see line 3190). This makes the condition always false and calls `stopAutoRefresh()`, violating expected behavior that auto-refresh works with progressive flow.

`cancelInProgressEventSource()` closes the previous EventSource without settling its promise. Since resolution paths are only in event handlers (`done`/`onerror`), a canceled stream can leave the prior request pending indefinitely (relevant to overlapping manual compare vs in-flight auto-refresh).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fullstack-worker skill prescribes tests-first (red phase), but transcript order shows major `src/server.ts` implementation edits before adding the new `/compare-stream` tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton: multiple `Edit` operations to `src/server.ts` occur before the later `Edit` to `src/__tests__/server.quote-compare.test.ts` that adds SSE tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Address truncation guidance appears ambiguous in practice: AGENTS says never truncate addresses, but new compare-stream logging truncates with `.slice(0, 10)`.

summary: Review failed. Progressive SSE behavior is mostly implemented, but auto-refresh integration regresses due to missing payload propagation in the new request path, and stream-cancel lifecycle handling may leave pending promises.
